channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy loses primary. And, WHO declares Ebola outbreak a global emergency
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy, who voted to convict Trump, lost the Republican primary in Louisiana. And, the WHO has declared a global health emergency over a new Ebola outbreak.
item.content:encoded:
Sen. Bill Cassidy, who voted to convict Trump, lost the Republican primary in Louisiana. And, the WHO has declared a global health emergency over a new Ebola outbreak.

(Image credit: Tyler Kaufman)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Why catching insider trading is so tricky nowadays, and just how helpful is it for kids to sleep in?
item.description: Millions of dollars have been made through eerily well-timed bets on prediction markets like Polymarket. We look at why they're so hard to police. And, a new study that supports kids sleeping in.
item.content:encoded: Millions of dollars have been made through eerily well-timed bets on prediction markets like Polymarket. We look at why they're so hard to police. And, a new study that supports kids sleeping in.
item.dc:creator: Cooper Katz McKim

item.title: Pop star Shakira is acquitted in a Spanish tax fraud case
item.description: The decision follows years of tax troubles in Spain for the Colombian superstar. Spanish tax authorities did not prove that the singer was a resident of Spain, the court said in its decision.
item.content:encoded:
The decision follows years of tax troubles in Spain for the Colombian superstar. Spanish tax authorities did not prove that the singer was a resident of Spain, the court said in its decision.

(Image credit: Charles Sykes)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Thousands of U.S. countertop workers could have damaged lungs, safety expert says
item.description: Over 550 men in California have fallen ill after cutting natural or factory-made stone countertops. But epidemiologists say this isn't just a California problem.
item.content:encoded:
Over 550 men in California have fallen ill after cutting natural or factory-made stone countertops. But epidemiologists say this isn't just a California problem.

(Image credit: Tina Russell for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: Why the Supreme Court's voting rights ruling could play a big role at the local level
item.description: The Supreme Court's recent ruling threatens the power of racial-minority voters in Voting Rights Act cases about not just Congress, but also at least 17 state and local governments, NPR finds.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court's recent ruling threatens the power of racial-minority voters in Voting Rights Act cases about not just Congress, but also at least 17 state and local governments, NPR finds.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: For 20 years, Stephen Colbert distinguished truth from truthiness
item.description: The Late Show with Stephen Colbert ends on Thursday. Here's how he has evolved to meet the moment.
item.content:encoded:
The Colbert Report in December 2014.'/>

The Late Show with Stephen Colbert ends on Thursday. Here's how he has evolved to meet the moment.

(Image credit: Andrew Harrer)
item.dc:creator: Eric Deggans

item.title: Montana tribes combine traditional knowledge and Western science in climate plan
item.description: The Confederated Salish and Kootenai Tribes are moving forward with their climate plan despite the loss of state support and federal funding.
item.content:encoded:
The Confederated Salish and Kootenai Tribes are moving forward with their climate plan despite the loss of state support and federal funding.

(Image credit: Ryan Kellman)
item.dc:creator: Ellis Juhlin

item.title: In Georgia primary, Republicans dominate spending and Democrats drive record turnout
item.description: Tuesday's primary in Georgia features contentious Republican contests for governor and U.S. Senate while Democrats hope an enthusiasm advantage is enough to flip two state supreme court seats.
item.content:encoded:
Tuesday's primary in Georgia features contentious Republican contests for governor and U.S. Senate while Democrats hope an enthusiasm advantage is enough to flip two state supreme court seats.

(Image credit: Brynn Anderson)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: It takes a village – or a Phoenix suburb – to wrangle a wayward tortoise
item.description: When a large tortoise named Rex got loose, a Phoenix-area neighborhood went into a tizzy. More than just a fun commotion, Rex's daring getaway shows the challenges of sulcata tortoise ownership.
item.content:encoded:
When a large tortoise named Rex got loose, a Phoenix-area neighborhood went into a tizzy. More than just a fun commotion, Rex's daring getaway shows the challenges of sulcata tortoise ownership.

(Image credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Alina Hartounian

item.title: They've got fiber. They're cheap. They're … cool? Behind the 'renaissance' of beans
item.description: The fiber craze is pushing more people into the broad world of beans, as the U.S. bean industry looks to double American consumption of pulses by 2030.
item.content:encoded: The fiber craze is pushing more people into the broad world of beans, as the U.S. bean industry looks to double American consumption of pulses by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez
